prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "跳跃弓步" (jumping lunge) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person at the bottom of a jumping lunge just before takeoff: front leg (right) bent at 90 degrees with thigh parallel to the floor, foot flat; rear leg (left) bent at 90 degrees with knee just above the ground, ball of the rear foot on the ground; torso upright with neutral spine, arms swinging in running-style opposition (left arm forward, right arm back); plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: quadriceps and gluteus maximus of the front leg, calf muscles for jump propulsion. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: hamstring group and hip flexors. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "股四头肌" pointing to the quadriceps of the front leg; "臀大肌" pointing to the gluteus maximus; "小腿三头肌 / 腘绳肌" pointing to the calf and hamstring area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
